steps.title: Convert the area unit
steps.content: We need to express 1 cm² in mm². Since 1 cm = 10 mm, squaring both sides gives: $$1 \text{ cm}^2 = (10 \text{ mm})^2 = 100 \text{ mm}^2$$

steps.title: Calculate density in g/mm²
steps.content: The given density is 0.812 g/cm², meaning 0.812 grams per 1 cm². Since 1 cm² = 100 mm², this is equivalent to 0.812 g per 100 mm², or: $$\frac{0.812}{100} = 0.00812 \text{ g/mm}^2$$ This matches choice D — D is correct.
